steps.title: Use the cornflower intercept
steps.content: The point $(16,0)$ means the total is spent on $16$ cornflowers and $0$ wallflowers. So $16$ cornflowers cost $24.00$ dollars total.

steps.title: Find one cornflower price
steps.content:
Divide the total cost by the number of cornflowers:
$$\frac{24}{16}=\frac{3}{2}$$
The answer is 1.5.
